articles.title: Iran War Updates: U.S. carries out 10th night of strikes after 3 U.S. troops killed, nearly 100 injured in recent weeks - CBS News
articles.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

articles.title: Trump says he will impose 50 percent tariffs on Canadian goods in 30 days - The Washington Post
articles.description: The president said the levies will take effect in 30 days unless Canada drops what the administration called “discriminatory” trade barriers against U.S. products.

articles.title: Tropical Storm Bertha forms in the Gulf, forecasts warn of flooding - USA Today
articles.description: Tropical Storm Bertha formed on July 20 as it moved toward the Gulf Coast, where forecasters say it could bring flash flooding to multiple states.

articles.title: Judge, nominated by Trump, rules against him in key Minnesota case - The Hill
articles.description: A Trump-nominated federal judge has ruled against the administration in a key case centered around local immigration policy in Minnesota. According to a Monday filing, the Trump administration asserted that Minnesota sanctuary laws violate the Supremacy Claus…

articles.title: The New Mercedes-Maybach GLS Debuts With More Power, Better Style - Motor1.com
articles.description: Mercedes-Benz has unveiled the 2027 Maybach GLS 680, which now has a more powerful twin-turbocharged V8 engine and an updated design. These are the details.

articles.title: US soldiers killed by Iran were from Texas and Hawaii, military says - AP News
articles.description: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in action last week in the war with Iran. The Defense Department said Monday in a statement that 25-year-old 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and 19-year-old Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, of Carr…

articles.title: 'Anti-ICE' Army veteran set fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials - BBC
articles.description: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

articles.title: Box Office Global: How Other Films Ranked In 'Odyssey' Heavy Weekend - Deadline
articles.description: After 'The Odyssey', China's Stephen Chow movie 'Kung Fu Soccer' ruled the world. In addition Cannes Sci-Fi movie 'Hope' opened.

articles.title: Guardians' offense enjoys breakout game with 7-homer night - ESPN
articles.description: The Guardians, who came Monday's game with the third-fewest homers in the majors, socked seven of them in a 13-4 rout of the Twins.

articles.title: ESPN reportedly lays off analyst Ryan Clark during broadcast of 'NFL Live' - Yahoo Sports
articles.description: Clark became the first high-profile victim of a round of layoffs that is reportedly set to take place on Tuesday, per The Athletic.

articles.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test - NPR
articles.description: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.

articles.title: Fourth person dies from Legionnaires’ disease outbreak in Manhattan - Yahoo
articles.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) — A fourth person has died in connection with Legionnaires’ disease in Manhattan, according to the city’s health department. The first reported death in relation to the Legionnaires cluster on the Upper East Side was reported on Friday…

articles.title: ‘King of the North’ Andy Burnham becomes 7th British prime minister in a decade - NBC News
articles.description: The new Labour Party leader, Andy Burnham, replaced Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.

articles.title: Women with common health condition face higher risk ofheart disease - The Times
articles.description: Polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, which affects one in eight women in the UK, increases the chances of developing cardiovascular disease fourfold, a study finds

articles.title: ‘I’ve Been Scammed Left and Right’: Demand for ‘Odyssey’ Tickets Gives Rise to Sketchy Scalpers - Variety
articles.description: 'The Odyssey' is only playing on one Imax 70mm screen in New York City, and sold out shows have brought out aggressive scalpers and scammers.

articles.title: Douglas County officials: 'Rapid' Cyclospora surge reported, Taco Bell emerges as top exposure - KETV
articles.description: Officials said Douglas County epidemiologists have identified eating at Taco Bell as the most common exposure for the cases reported in the area.
